expectations.text: The review identifies the test file importing from '../src/internal/helpers' as a test boundary violation
expectations.evidence: Report [Important]: 'tests target internals... tests import ../src/internal/helpers directly and do not cover... changed service-level edge cases.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the comment 'Used by the UserRegistration component in pages/register.tsx and the AdminPanel' as upstream knowledge that will rot
expectations.evidence: Refactoring Changes: 'removed transient/usage-context comments' and 'removed non-essential inline comments to reduce drift-prone commentary.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the 'export * from ./internal/helpers' in index.ts as leaking internal implementation details through the barrel
expectations.evidence: Report [Minor]: 'internal helpers are now part of public package surface... export * from ./internal/helpers exposes internals that were previously non-public.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the constructor doing require('pg') and new Client() as a DI anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Report flags require('pg') as ESM runtime crash risk [Critical] and undeclared dependency [Important], but frames it as module-format and package.json issues, not as an architectural boundary/DI violation. Mentions 'injection' as part of fix suggestions but does not identify the root cause as coupling business logic to infrastructure.

expectations.text: The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)
expectations.evidence: Report uses **[Critical]**, **[Important]**, **[Minor]** with file:line, Evidence, Recommendation throughout.
